purpose: Declarative architecture conformance rules for the G1 gate (spec-00). Each rule is tagged HARD or ADVISORY HERE, in data — never decided at runtime by the implementing agent (closes the gate-weakening backdoor the review flagged). HARD rules gate ship; ADVISORY rules score+report.
note: These rules target THIS repo (Python lgwks modules). The canvas-motion Rust ER model referenced earlier does not yet exist in-repo; when it lands, add its layering rules here under a second ruleset. Until then G1 operates on the rules below.
false_pass_surface: Static import/AST scanning has known false negatives: dynamic imports (importlib), global state via closures/class attributes, and macro-equivalent metaprogramming evade the scan. Rules whose false-PASS surface cannot be bounded are tagged ADVISORY, not HARD (per spec-01 soundness obligation).

rules.why: T0 data-boundary: the three trust-boundary stores (untrusted cache, audit cognition, encrypted vault) must never import a network surface. A store that can reach the network can exfiltrate. AST import-edge check is complete for static imports → HARD.
rules.diagnosis_hint: found a network import in a data-boundary module; route network through a caller, not the store

rules.why: Layering: the Verifier primitive (spec-01) is the foundation; gates depend on it, never the reverse. A cycle here means the primitive knows its consumers → un-testable in isolation. Static check → HARD.
rules.diagnosis_hint: lgwks_verify must not import any gate; invert the dependency

rules.why: Determinism + statelessness (T4): core gate modules must hold no mutable module-level state. ADVISORY not HARD because interior mutability via closures/class attributes evades a static scan — false-PASS surface is unbounded, so this scores+warns, it does not certify.
rules.diagnosis_hint: module-level mutable binding detected; move state into an explicit parameter or a frozen value

rules.forbid_pattern: except handler whose body is only `pass` or only a bare `...`
rules.why: Typed errors / no silent failure (T4). ADVISORY because intent of an empty except can be legitimate (documented graceful-degrade); the gate flags every instance for review rather than blocking, since it cannot read intent.
rules.diagnosis_hint: empty except swallows a typed error; return a Verdict/Result or re-raise with a code
